questions.text: Who was the first Indian woman to write an autobiography, titled 'Amar Jiban'?
questions.explanation: Rashsundari Devi was a housewife from a rich landlord's family who learned to read and write in secret.

questions.text: Pandita Ramabai was given the title 'Pandita' because she could read and write:
questions.explanation: She was a remarkable achievement as women then were not allowed such knowledge; she set up a Mission in Khedgaon.

questions.text: What was the title of the story written by Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain in 1905 while she was learning English?
questions.explanation: The story imagined a place called Ladyland where women had the freedom to study and invent.

questions.text: International Women's Day is celebrated globally on:
questions.explanation: On March 8, women all over the world come together to celebrate their struggles and solidarity.

questions.text: Which method of the women's movement involves creating public awareness through songs, plays, and meetings?
questions.explanation: Spreading awareness is a key part of the women's movement to sensitize the public on women's rights.

questions.text: In the 2011 census, what was the literacy rate for women in India?
questions.explanation: While literacy has increased, the gap between male (82%) and female (65%) literacy still exists.

questions.text: Campaigning is an important part of the women's movement to fight:
questions.explanation: Campaigns have led to new laws being passed, such as the law against domestic violence in 2006.

questions.text: What is the primary reason why many girls from Dalit and Adivasi backgrounds leave school?
questions.explanation: A combination of lack of facilities in rural areas and socio-economic pressures leads to higher dropout rates.

options: Show support for other women and causes
questions.explanation: Solidarity is about showing unity with other women and shared global struggles.

questions.text: Satya Rani, an active member of the women's movement, fought specifically against:
questions.explanation: She sat on the steps of the Supreme Court seeking justice for her daughter who was murdered for dowry.
